sections.paragraph:
ἱκανότης, ητος, ἡ state of being qualified or adequate for someth., fitness, capability, qualification (s. ἱκανός Lysias, Fgm. 211 Sauppe [in Pollux 4, 23]; Pla., Lysis 11 p. 215a; Stoic. III 68, 3) 2 Cor 3:5.—DELG s.v. ἵκω. TW. Spicq.

someth. someth. = something

Lysias Lysias , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Pollux Pollux , II a.d.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Lysis Lysis , V b.c.; for writings attributed to him, IV/II b.c., s. Vorsokr. 46;—List 5

Stoic Stoic , various dates—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 473
